claims.claimText: Shared hand-wrist anatomy (second-metacarpal, third-metacarpal, fourth-metacarpal) links Metacarpal Fracture to adjacent Prepare neighborhoods in the cluster graph.

metadata.claim_text: Shared hand-wrist anatomy (second-metacarpal, third-metacarpal, fourth-metacarpal) links Metacarpal Fracture to adjacent Prepare neighborhoods in the cluster graph.

claims.claimText: Missing second-metacarpal assessment in metacarpal fracture is a common board and OITE trap.

metadata.claim_text: Missing second-metacarpal assessment in metacarpal fracture is a common board and OITE trap.

claims.claimText: Document neurovascular status, key anatomic structures (second-metacarpal, third-metacarpal, fourth-metacarpal), stability assessment, and escalation plan for metacarpal fracture.

metadata.claim_text: Document neurovascular status, key anatomic structures (second-metacarpal, third-metacarpal, fourth-metacarpal), stability assessment, and escalation plan for metacarpal fracture.

claims.claimText: Treating metacarpal fracture in isolation without checking related wrist, tendon, or nerve neighborhoods in the cluster.

metadata.claim_text: Treating metacarpal fracture in isolation without checking related wrist, tendon, or nerve neighborhoods in the cluster.

claims.claimText: Obtain appropriate hand/wrist imaging for metacarpal fracture and explicitly comment on articular alignment and adjacent joint stability.

metadata.claim_text: Obtain appropriate hand/wrist imaging for metacarpal fracture and explicitly comment on articular alignment and adjacent joint stability.

claims.claimText: Metacarpal Fracture evaluation should anchor on mechanism, focused exam, and the anatomic structures most at risk (second-metacarpal, third-metacarpal, fourth-metacarpal).

metadata.claim_text: Metacarpal Fracture evaluation should anchor on mechanism, focused exam, and the anatomic structures most at risk (second-metacarpal, third-metacarpal, fourth-metacarpal).

claims.claimText: Early protected motion and therapy goals for metacarpal fracture should align with tendon, ligament, and joint stability constraints.

metadata.claim_text: Early protected motion and therapy goals for metacarpal fracture should align with tendon, ligament, and joint stability constraints.

decisionPoints.trigger: Stable pattern with acceptable alignment and intact neurovascular exam for metacarpal fracture
decisionPoints.action: Splint/immobilize with therapy plan, repeat exam and imaging, and clear return precautions

decisionPoints.trigger: Unstable pattern, displacement, failed closed treatment, or functional deficit in metacarpal fracture
decisionPoints.action: Proceed with operative pathway when indicated; document fixation strategy, tendon/nerve protection, and postoperative plan
